title: Philippine Pavilion
creator: Ar. Leandro Locsin
description: The dramatic roof sweeping up from the ground was intended to express the soaring prospects and future-oriented outlook of the Filipino people. The architectural message was that although the Philippines is a young and developing country, it has a progressive spirit.
subject: Pavilion

title: Cultural Center of The Philippines(CCP)
creator: Ar. Leandro Locsin
description: Cultural Center Of the Philippines(CCP) is a premier example of Philippine Brutalism and tropical modernism. Located on 88 hectares of reclaimed land, it features massive, geometric concrete structures designed to appear as if they are floating, characterized by heavy use of cantilevered forms, concrete cast-in-place, and a, "floating volume" effect
subject: Cultural Complex
location: Roxas Boulevard, Pasay City, Metro Manila, Philippines

title: Philippine International Convention Center(PICC)
creator: Ar. Leandro Locsin
description: The PICC complex is renowned for its massive, raw concrete forms, geometric precision, and a design that blends monumental exterior strength with warm, functional interiors
subject: Convention Center
location: CCP Complex, Pasay City, Metro Manila

title: Tanghalang Maria Makiling
creator: Ar. Leandro Locsin
description: It is a masterpiece of neo-vernacular architecture, reinterpreting the traditional Filipino nipa hut through a modernist lens, featuring a tent-like structure with concrete bleacher seats that accommodate up to 1,000 guests
location: Mount Makiling, Los Baños, Laguna, Philippines

title: Benguet Center(Banco De Oro Bldg.)
creator: Ar. Leandro Locsin
description: The Benguet Center is a fine example of Brutalism,  like most structures designed and constructed in this style, is suggestive of a massive sculpture - with large swathes of horizontal bands of concrete slabs interspersed by vertical concrete blocks in varying heights faced with roughly hewn dark Benguet stones that convey a stark and austere rectilinearity.
location: ADB Avenue corner Julia Vargas Avenue, Ortigas Center, Mandaluyong City,
